rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical epoxidation of styrenes and aliphatic alkenes using (TMP)MnCl catalyst with H2O as oxygen source under constant current electrolysis
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Mn(III) catalyst to a high-valent Mn-oxo species using H2O as the oxygen atom source; the Mn-oxo then transfers oxygen to the alkene

key_intermediates.name: high-valent Mn-oxo (Mn(V)=O or Mn(IV)=O) species derived from H2O

electron_flow: At the anode, the (TMP)Mn(III) catalyst is oxidized in the presence of H2O (and pyridine base) to generate a high-valent Mn-oxo intermediate; this electrophilic oxo transfers its oxygen atom to the alkene to give the epoxide, regenerating the lower-valent Mn for re-oxidation at the anode. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O (isotope-labeled water)
probe_reagents.observation: GC-MS analysis of epoxide product from electrolysis with H2-18O confirms that the oxygen atom incorporated into the epoxide originates from water (Figure S1)

notes: Only the isotope-labeling page is shown here; full mechanistic picture (e.g., CV, radical traps, DFT) may appear elsewhere in the SI. The scale-up (3 mmol, 71% yield) confirms practicality. Pyridine likely serves as an axial ligand/base assisting Mn-oxo formation.
